finding: Workflow target access never re-checked (find_by_id is id-scoped only); any user can run/test-fire another user's or an unassigned system workflow via a scheduled task (IDOR).

A deleted target (workflow/model/bound conversation) is not auto-paused on first fire; the task re-fires each cadence sending N interrupting failure notifications until the max-failures cap, contradicting the migration-133 docs.
run_once awaits fire_task sequentially; a task whose dispatch hangs (up to 15min TERMINAL_WAIT) starves the whole scheduler — other users' due tasks make no progress (availability).

run-now calls record_outcome unconditionally, mutating consecutive_failures (can auto-pause) + the change-detection signature (rebases on_change), despite the handler contract that run-now doesn't touch schedule bookkeeping.
validate_schedule checks a single now-dependent gap; an uneven multi-time cron (e.g. 0,1 0 * * *) can slip past the min-interval floor and fire twice 60s apart daily.
A 409 (generation-in-progress in the bound conversation, e.g. user chatting there) is classified Internal/terminal → spurious failure notification + counts toward auto-pause; should be transient.
assistant_id ownership not validated at create/dispatch/test-fire (defense-in-depth: a scheduled task could inject a foreign assistant's system prompt depending on the downstream loader).
notify_mode/notify_on not validated in create/update; a bad value surfaces as a DB-CHECK 500 instead of a 400.

Claim comment claims next_run_at is advanced in the SAME transaction (DEC-16); it is not (fetch_all autocommits, advance is a separate statement). Misleading — corrected to reflect the single-process advance-before-spawn model.

Preset dropdown can never switch to Weekly/Monthly/Custom: dow/dom fall back with `|| '1'` which doesn't replace a truthy `*`, and preset is re-derived from cron every render, so a Daily cron re-classifies and the Select snaps back.
once run_at drifts by the UTC offset on every edit: stored UTC ISO is sliced and fed to a datetime-local input that renders/parses it as local time.
Bell popover reads the same Stores.Notifications.items the inbox page repaginates/filters; after visiting /notifications and paging/filtering, the bell shows the wrong (page-N / unread-only) slice.
once schedule has no run_at validation (submittable with empty run_at → generic server error) and clearing the datetime crashed the onChange with new Date('').toISOString().
